the.meaning: Definite article. Marks the noun as specific or previously mentioned. Always pre-nominal and invariant.

a.meaning: Indefinite article (before consonant sound). Marks singular countable noun as non-specific. Replaces 'an' before vowel sound.

an.meaning: Indefinite article (before vowel sound). Marks singular countable noun as non-specific. Replaces 'a' before consonant sound.

this.meaning: Demonstrative determiner/pronoun (singular, near). Can modify noun (this book) or stand alone (this is mine).

that.meaning: Demonstrative determiner/pronoun (singular, far). Can modify noun (that book) or stand alone (that is mine).

these.meaning: Demonstrative determiner/pronoun (plural, near). Can modify noun (these books) or stand alone (these are mine).

those.meaning: Demonstrative determiner/pronoun (plural, far). Can modify noun (those books) or stand alone (those are mine).

some.meaning: Indefinite quantifier. Indicates unknown/nonspecific quantity. Can modify noun (some books) or stand alone (some are red).

any.meaning: Universal quantifier (interrogative/negative contexts). Indicates no restriction. Any book works. Do you have any?

all.meaning: Universal quantifier. Indicates total quantity. All books are here. Precedes article or possessive (all the books).

each.meaning: Distributive quantifier. Indicates individual members separately. Each student has a book. Requires singular noun.

every.meaning: Distributive quantifier. Similar to 'each' but emphasizes universality. Every student arrived. Requires singular noun.

either.meaning: Two-way choice (positive contexts). Either option works. Requires singular noun. Correlative: either...or.

neither.meaning: Two-way negation. Neither option works. Requires singular noun. Correlative: neither...nor.

both.meaning: Two-way inclusion. Both options work. Requires plural noun or plural noun phrase. Both the books.

another.meaning: One more of the same kind (singular). Another book appeared. Presupposes at least one already mentioned.

other.meaning: Different/remaining (can be singular or plural). The other book. The other books. Often used with 'the'.

such.meaning: Of that kind/degree. Such books are rare. Precedes article in formal English (such a book).

what.meaning: Interrogative/relative pronoun. Asks for identity or thing. What is this? I know what you mean.

which.meaning: Interrogative/relative pronoun for choice. Which book do you prefer? The book which arrived yesterday.

who.meaning: Interrogative/relative pronoun for person (nominative). Who arrived? The man who came.

whom.meaning: Interrogative/relative pronoun for person (accusative/object). Whom did you see? The man whom I saw. (Formal/archaic)

whose.meaning: Interrogative/relative possessive pronoun. Whose book is this? The person whose book was lost.

I.meaning: 1st person singular subject pronoun (nominative case). I am happy.

me.meaning: 1st person singular object pronoun (accusative case). He saw me. Give me the book.

my.meaning: 1st person singular possessive determiner. Always pre-nominal. My book. My friends.

mine.meaning: 1st person singular possessive pronoun. Stands alone replacing noun. This book is mine.

myself.meaning: 1st person singular reflexive pronoun. Reflects back to subject. I hurt myself. I did it myself (emphatic).

you.meaning: 2nd person subject/object pronoun (nominative/accusative). Invariant for all contexts. You are happy. I see you.

your.meaning: 2nd person possessive determiner. Always pre-nominal. Your book. Your friends.

yours.meaning: 2nd person possessive pronoun. Stands alone. This book is yours.

yourself.meaning: 2nd person singular reflexive pronoun. You hurt yourself. Do it yourself.

yourselves.meaning: 2nd person plural reflexive pronoun. You hurt yourselves. Do it yourselves.

he.meaning: 3rd person masculine singular subject pronoun (nominative). He is happy.

him.meaning: 3rd person masculine singular object pronoun (accusative). I saw him. Give him the book.

his.meaning: 3rd person masculine singular possessive determiner/pronoun. Pre-nominal (his book) or standalone (the book is his).

himself.meaning: 3rd person masculine singular reflexive pronoun. He hurt himself. He did it himself.

she.meaning: 3rd person feminine singular subject pronoun (nominative). She is happy.

her.meaning: 3rd person feminine singular object pronoun (accusative). I saw her. Give her the book.

hers.meaning: 3rd person feminine singular possessive pronoun. Stands alone. This book is hers.

herself.meaning: 3rd person feminine singular reflexive pronoun. She hurt herself. She did it herself.

it.meaning: 3rd person neuter singular subject/object pronoun. I saw it. It is here. Also formal subject in existential (it is raining).

its.meaning: 3rd person neuter singular possessive determiner. Always pre-nominal. Its purpose. Its members.

itself.meaning: 3rd person neuter singular reflexive pronoun. The machine repaired itself. The book itself is valuable.

we.meaning: 1st person plural subject pronoun (nominative). We are happy.

us.meaning: 1st person plural object pronoun (accusative). He saw us. Give us the book.

our.meaning: 1st person plural possessive determiner. Always pre-nominal. Our book. Our friends.

ours.meaning: 1st person plural possessive pronoun. Stands alone. This book is ours.

ourselves.meaning: 1st person plural reflexive pronoun. We hurt ourselves. We did it ourselves.

they.meaning: 3rd person plural subject pronoun (nominative). They are happy. Also singular 'they' (gender-neutral).

them.meaning: 3rd person plural object pronoun (accusative). I saw them. Give them the book.

their.meaning: 3rd person plural possessive determiner. Always pre-nominal. Their book. Their friends.

theirs.meaning: 3rd person plural possessive pronoun. Stands alone. This book is theirs.

themselves.meaning: 3rd person plural reflexive pronoun. They hurt themselves. They did it themselves.

one.meaning: Indefinite pronoun for generic reference (formal). One must be careful. One's duty is important.

someone.meaning: Indefinite pronoun (positive). Someone is at the door. Someone has taken my book.

someone's.meaning: Possessive form of 'someone'. Someone's book was lost.

anybody.meaning: Indefinite pronoun (interrogative/negative/conditional). Does anybody know? Anybody can do this.

anybody's.meaning: Possessive form of 'anybody'. Is this anybody's coat?

anything.meaning: Indefinite pronoun (nonspecific thing, interrogative/negative). Do you want anything? I didn't see anything.

everyone.meaning: Indefinite pronoun (all people). Everyone is happy. Everyone has a choice.

everyone's.meaning: Possessive form of 'everyone'. Everyone's opinion matters.

everything.meaning: Indefinite pronoun (all things). Everything is ready. I did everything.

nobody.meaning: Indefinite pronoun (no person, negative). Nobody came. Nobody knows.

nobody's.meaning: Possessive form of 'nobody'. It's nobody's fault.

nothing.meaning: Indefinite pronoun (no thing, negative). Nothing happened. I saw nothing.

many.meaning: Indefinite pronoun/determiner (large quantity). Many people came. Many of them arrived.

few.meaning: Indefinite pronoun/determiner (small quantity). Few people came. A few were late.

some.meaning: Indefinite pronoun/determiner (nonspecific quantity). Some came. Some of them were late.

all.meaning: Indefinite pronoun/determiner (total quantity). All came. All of them were happy.

most.meaning: Indefinite pronoun/determiner (majority). Most people agree. Most of them arrived.

who.meaning: Relative pronoun for person (nominative/subject). The man who arrived is here.

whom.meaning: Relative pronoun for person (accusative/object). The man whom I met is here. (Formal/archaic)

which.meaning: Relative pronoun for things/animals. The book which I read was good. Restrictive or non-restrictive.

that.meaning: Relative pronoun for things/people. The book that I read was good. Typically restrictive; 'that' is often deletable.

whose.meaning: Relative possessive pronoun. The man whose book was lost is here.

because.meaning: Introduces reason/cause clause. I left because I was tired. Adverbial subordinator.

if.meaning: Introduces conditional clause. If you arrive early, we can leave. Both real and counterfactual conditionals.

unless.meaning: Introduces negative conditional. You will fail unless you study. Equivalent to 'if not'.

although.meaning: Introduces concessive clause. Although it rained, we went out. Grants truth of clause but emphasizes contrast.

though.meaning: Introduces concessive clause. Though it rained, we went out. More informal/archaic than 'although'.

when.meaning: Introduces temporal clause. When you arrive, call me. Also: simultaneous or sequential time.

while.meaning: Introduces temporal clause (simultaneous). While we were eating, it rained. Can also mean 'although' (concessive).

until.meaning: Introduces temporal clause (up to point). We waited until you arrived. Negative counterpart: before.

after.meaning: Introduces temporal clause (following). After you left, we went home. Presupposes completion of dependent event.

before.meaning: Introduces temporal clause (preceding). Before you go, call me. Presupposes non-occurrence of dependent event.

since.meaning: Introduces temporal clause (from point onward). Since you arrived, we've been happy. Also: causal (because).

as.meaning: Introduces temporal/causal/manner clause. As you know, it rained. As we entered, the lights turned on.

so_that.meaning: Introduces purpose clause. I studied so that I would pass. Expresses intention/goal.

in_order_that.meaning: Introduces purpose clause (formal). I studied in order that I would pass. Equivalent to 'so that'.

whereas.meaning: Introduces contrasting clause. Whereas you stayed, I left. Emphasizes difference between clauses.

provided_that.meaning: Introduces conditional clause (formal). You can go provided that you return early. Synonym: 'as long as'.

as_long_as.meaning: Introduces conditional clause. You can go as long as you return early. Synonym: 'provided that'.

and.meaning: Joins equal elements. Additive. John and Mary came. I read and wrote.

but.meaning: Joins equal elements. Contrastive. John came but Mary didn't. I tried but failed.

or.meaning: Joins equal elements. Disjunctive (exclusive or inclusive). John or Mary will come. Tea or coffee?

nor.meaning: Joins equal elements. Negative disjunction. John didn't come, nor did Mary. Neither...nor correlative.

yet.meaning: Joins equal elements. Contrastive (yet suggests surprise). I tried yet failed. Similar to 'but' but stronger.

so.meaning: Joins equal elements. Consequential. I was tired, so I left. Informal equivalent to 'therefore'.

for.meaning: Joins equal elements. Causal (archaic/formal). John didn't come, for he was ill. Rarely used in modern English.

in.meaning: Marks location (inside) or temporal frame. In the box. In summer. In the morning.

on.meaning: Marks location (surface) or temporal (day/date). On the table. On Monday. On January 1st.

at.meaning: Marks location (point) or time (specific moment). At the store. At 3 o'clock. At home.

to.meaning: Marks direction/goal. I go to school. From 9 to 5. Also: indirect object (give the book to her). NOT infinitive marker (different function).

from.meaning: Marks origin/source. I come from France. Made from wood. From 9 to 5.

with.meaning: Marks accompaniment or instrument. I went with my friend. Cut it with a knife.

without.meaning: Marks absence or lack. I went without my friend. Without help, I failed.

by.meaning: Marks agent (passive voice), instrument, or proximity. Written by Shakespeare. Travel by car. By the window.

for.meaning: Marks beneficiary, purpose, or duration. This is for you. A knife for cutting. I've lived here for 5 years.

of.meaning: Marks possession, composition, or part-whole. The book of John. A cup of coffee. Member of the group.

about.meaning: Marks topic or approximate time/amount. A book about Paris. About 10 people. About 3 o'clock.

above.meaning: Marks location (higher). The picture is above the table. Also: figuratively (above the law).

below.meaning: Marks location (lower). The picture is below the table. Also: figuratively (below average).

under.meaning: Marks location (underneath) or authority. Under the table. Under the law. Under his supervision.

over.meaning: Marks location (above, crossing) or duration. Over the hill. The plane flew over us. Over several years.

through.meaning: Marks movement (inside space) or agency. Through the forest. Through hard work. Through the entire year.

between.meaning: Marks location (two things). Between you and me. Between Monday and Friday.

among.meaning: Marks location (multiple things). Among the crowd. It is among them. Distributes over plural/mass.

behind.meaning: Marks location (back). Behind the door. Behind schedule (figuratively: late).

before.meaning: Marks spatial (in front) or temporal (earlier). Before the door. Before noon. Appeared before the judge.

after.meaning: Marks temporal (later) or following. After lunch. After you. He came after me.

during.meaning: Marks temporal (throughout event). During the summer. During the meeting.

toward.meaning: Marks direction/goal. He walked toward the door. Toward evening. Also: attitude (friendly toward them).

towards.meaning: British variant of 'toward'. He walked towards the door.

near.meaning: Marks proximity. Near the store. Near the end. Also: approximation (near 5 o'clock).

beside.meaning: Marks location (alongside). Beside the road. Sit beside me.

besides.meaning: Marks addition (in addition to). Besides that, I was tired. Besides the books, I bought pens.

except.meaning: Marks exclusion. Everyone except John came. All except for the last one.

against.meaning: Marks opposition or contact. Against the law. Lean against the wall. Argument against his proposal.

within.meaning: Marks location (inside) or temporal (before deadline). Within the building. Within a month.

throughout.meaning: Marks duration/distribution. Throughout the year. Throughout the country.

along.meaning: Marks direction (lengthwise) or accompaniment. Walk along the path. Along with my friend, I went.

across.meaning: Marks movement (crosswise) or location. Across the street. The house across from mine.

around.meaning: Marks location (encircling) or approximation. Around the table. Around 10 o'clock. Around the world.

down.meaning: Marks direction (downward) or along. Down the street. Down the hill. Also phrasal verb particle.

up.meaning: Marks direction (upward) or along. Up the street. Up the hill. Also phrasal verb particle.

into.meaning: Marks movement/change into enclosed space or state. Go into the room. Turn into a frog. Inquire into the matter.

onto.meaning: Marks movement onto surface. Jump onto the table. Also: awareness (it dawned onto him).

per.meaning: Marks distribution or relation. Per day. Per capita. Per instructions.

via.meaning: Marks route/medium. Via email. Via the northern route. I heard via her.

like.meaning: Marks similarity or manner. Similar to. Like his father. Acts like a child. (Also: verb, different function)

unlike.meaning: Marks dissimilarity. Unlike his father. Unlike most people, he succeeded.

be.meaning: Auxiliary verb. Forms progressive aspect (is running) and passive voice (is eaten). Base form of copula.

am.meaning: 1st person singular present of 'be'. I am happy. I am running.

are.meaning: 2nd person singular/plural present of 'be'. You are happy. We are running.

is.meaning: 3rd person singular present of 'be'. He is happy. She is running.

was.meaning: 1st/3rd person singular past of 'be'. I was happy. She was running.

were.meaning: 2nd person singular/plural past of 'be'. You were happy. We were running.

been.meaning: Past participle of 'be'. Used in perfect (have been) and passives (has been eaten).

being.meaning: Present participle of 'be'. In progressive (is being eaten) or nominal (Being happy is important).

have.meaning: Auxiliary verb. Forms perfect aspect (have eaten). Base form.

has.meaning: 3rd person singular present of 'have'. She has eaten. He has lived here.

had.meaning: Past of 'have'. She had eaten. We had left before noon.

having.meaning: Present participle of 'have'. In perfect (having eaten) or nominal uses.

do.meaning: Auxiliary verb. Marks emphatic, interrogative, or negative clauses. Base form. I do go. Do you go?

does.meaning: 3rd person singular present of 'do'. Does she go? He does like it.

did.meaning: Past of 'do'. Did you go? She did finish it.

doing.meaning: Present participle of 'do'. In emphatic (she is doing her best) or nominal uses.

can.meaning: Modal expressing ability/possibility. Can you swim? He can do it.

could.meaning: Past/hypothetical of 'can' or politeness. I could swim. Could you help? Could you pass the salt? (polite).

will.meaning: Modal expressing future prediction/intention. She will arrive. I will go tomorrow.

would.meaning: Conditional/hypothetical of 'will' or politeness. I would go if I could. Would you help me?

shall.meaning: Modal (archaic for future). Shall I go? I shall return. Mostly used in formal British English or legal texts.

should.meaning: Modal expressing obligation/advice/expectation. You should go. It should work. He should have arrived.

may.meaning: Modal expressing possibility/permission. It may rain. May I enter? Formal permission.

might.meaning: Modal expressing possibility (weaker than 'may'). It might rain. She might have forgotten. Remote possibility.

must.meaning: Modal expressing obligation/logical necessity. You must go. He must be here (logical deduction).

ought.meaning: Modal expressing obligation (formal/archaic). You ought to go. He ought to have known.

go.meaning: Move from one place to another. I go to school. She goes every day. Goes, went, gone.

come.meaning: Move toward speaker/deictic center. Come here! She comes tomorrow. Comes, came, come.

get.meaning: Obtain/become/arrive. Get a book. Get older. Get home. Gets, got, got/gotten.

make.meaning: Produce/cause. Make a cake. Make him happy. Makes, made, made.

take.meaning: Grab/consume/require. Take my hand. Take a rest. Takes 5 minutes. Takes, took, taken.

give.meaning: Transfer to recipient. Give me the book. Gives, gave, given.

say.meaning: Utter words/express. Say hello. The sign says stop. Says, said, said.

tell.meaning: Communicate/narrate. Tell me the story. Tell them to go. Tells, told, told.

do.meaning: Perform action. Do your homework. What are you doing? Does, did, done.

know.meaning: Have knowledge/be acquainted with. Know the answer. Know her. Knows, knew, known.

think.meaning: Have thoughts/believe. I think so. Think about it. Thinks, thought, thought.

want.meaning: Desire. Do you want coffee? Wants, wanted, wanted.

see.meaning: Perceive with eyes/understand. See the tree. See what you mean. Sees, saw, seen.

look.meaning: Direct gaze/appear. Look at me. You look tired. Looks, looked, looked.

find.meaning: Discover/locate. Find your keys. Finds, found, found.

use.meaning: Employ/utilize. Use a pen. Uses, used, used.

work.meaning: Labor/function. Work hard. This plan works. Works, worked, worked.

call.meaning: Summon/name/telephone. Call me. They call him John. Calls, called, called.

ask.meaning: Request/pose question. Ask for help. Ask me why. Asks, asked, asked.

need.meaning: Require. I need a pen. Needs, needed, needed.

feel.meaning: Experience sensation/emotion. Feel tired. Feels, felt, felt.

try.meaning: Attempt. Try again. Tries, tried, tried.

leave.meaning: Depart/abandon/remain. Leave now. Leave me alone. Leaves, left, left.

put.meaning: Place in position. Put it there. Puts, put, put.

mean.meaning: Intend/signify. What do you mean? What does this word mean? Means, meant, meant.

keep.meaning: Retain/continue. Keep it. Keep going. Keeps, kept, kept.

let.meaning: Allow/permit. Let me go. Let's go. Lets, let, let.

begin.meaning: Start. Begin the work. Begins, began, begun.

seem.meaning: Appear. Seems right. Seems, seemed, seemed.

help.meaning: Assist. Help me. Helps, helped, helped.

talk.meaning: Speak. Talk to me. Talks, talked, talked.

turn.meaning: Rotate/transform. Turn around. Turn into a frog. Turns, turned, turned.

start.meaning: Begin/commence. Start work. Starts, started, started.

show.meaning: Exhibit/demonstrate. Show me. Shows, showed, shown.

hear.meaning: Perceive with ears. Hear me. Hears, heard, heard.

play.meaning: Engage in activity/perform music. Play a game. Play the piano. Plays, played, played.

run.meaning: Move quickly. Run fast. Runs, ran, run.

move.meaning: Change position. Move the chair. Moves, moved, moved.

live.meaning: Reside/exist. Live here. Lives, lived, lived.

believe.meaning: Accept as true. Believe the story. Believes, believed, believed.

happen.meaning: Occur/take place. What happened? Happens, happened, happened.

write.meaning: Compose text. Write a letter. Writes, wrote, written.

read.meaning: Interpret written text. Read a book. Reads, read, read.

wait.meaning: Remain in expectation. Wait here. Waits, waited, waited.

stop.meaning: Cease movement/action. Stop running. Stops, stopped, stopped.

follow.meaning: Go behind/comply. Follow me. Follows, followed, followed.

watch.meaning: Observe. Watch the movie. Watches, watched, watched.

pass.meaning: Go by/succeed in. Pass the exam. Passes, passed, passed.

open.meaning: Make accessible. Open the door. Opens, opened, opened.

close.meaning: Shut. Close the door. Closes, closed, closed.

speak.meaning: Utter words. Speak clearly. Speaks, spoke, spoken.

meet.meaning: Encounter. Meet me here. Meets, met, met.

become.meaning: Come to be. Become happy. Becomes, became, become.

sit.meaning: Be seated. Sit down. Sits, sat, sat.

stand.meaning: Be upright. Stand up. Stands, stood, stood.

lie.meaning: Recline/tell falsehood. Lie down. Lies, lay, lain.

appear.meaning: Seem/come into view. Appear happy. Appears, appeared, appeared.

carry.meaning: Transport. Carry the box. Carries, carried, carried.

build.meaning: Construct. Build a house. Builds, built, built.

cut.meaning: Divide with blade. Cut the paper. Cuts, cut, cut.

fall.meaning: Drop downward. Fall down. Falls, fell, fallen.

eat.meaning: Consume food. Eat bread. Eats, ate, eaten.

drink.meaning: Consume liquid. Drink water. Drinks, drank, drunk.

drive.meaning: Operate vehicle. Drive a car. Drives, drove, driven.

sell.meaning: Exchange for money. Sell the house. Sells, sold, sold.

buy.meaning: Purchase. Buy a book. Buys, bought, bought.

pay.meaning: Give money. Pay the bill. Pays, paid, paid.

win.meaning: Succeed in competition. Win the race. Wins, won, won.

lose.meaning: Misplace/fail to win. Lose the game. Loses, lost, lost.

break.meaning: Fracture/damage. Break the glass. Breaks, broke, broken.

fix.meaning: Repair. Fix the car. Fixes, fixed, fixed.

pull.meaning: Draw toward. Pull the rope. Pulls, pulled, pulled.

push.meaning: Shove away. Push the button. Pushes, pushed, pushed.

grow.meaning: Increase in size. Grow tall. Grows, grew, grown.

change.meaning: Become different. Change your mind. Changes, changed, changed.

spend.meaning: Use money/time. Spend money. Spends, spent, spent.

raise.meaning: Lift up/rear children. Raise your hand. Raises, raised, raised.

drop.meaning: Let fall. Drop the ball. Drops, dropped, dropped.

catch.meaning: Grab/contract illness. Catch the ball. Catches, caught, caught.

throw.meaning: Propel through air. Throw the ball. Throws, threw, thrown.

fight.meaning: Combat/struggle. Fight for justice. Fights, fought, fought.

teach.meaning: Instruct. Teach me. Teaches, taught, taught.

learn.meaning: Acquire knowledge. Learn quickly. Learns, learned/learnt, learned/learnt.

explain.meaning: Make clear. Explain the problem. Explains, explained, explained.

understand.meaning: Comprehend. Understand the meaning. Understands, understood, understood.

walk.meaning: Move on foot. Walk slowly. Walks, walked, walked.

jump.meaning: Leap. Jump high. Jumps, jumped, jumped.

dream.meaning: Have dreams/aspire. Dream of success. Dreams, dreamed/dreamt, dreamed/dreamt.

smile.meaning: Form smile on face. Smile at me. Smiles, smiled, smiled.

laugh.meaning: Express amusement. Laugh at the joke. Laughs, laughed, laughed.

cry.meaning: Shed tears/shout. Cry about it. Cries, cried, cried.

sing.meaning: Produce musical sounds. Sing a song. Sings, sang, sung.

dance.meaning: Move rhythmically. Dance to music. Dances, danced, danced.

draw.meaning: Create picture. Draw a sketch. Draws, drew, drawn.

paint.meaning: Apply color. Paint the wall. Paints, painted, painted.

cook.meaning: Prepare food by heat. Cook dinner. Cooks, cooked, cooked.

wash.meaning: Clean with water. Wash the car. Washes, washed, washed.

clean.meaning: Make clean. Clean the room. Cleans, cleaned, cleaned.

sleep.meaning: Rest unconsciously. Sleep well. Sleeps, slept, slept.

wake.meaning: Cease sleeping. Wake up. Wakes, woke, woken.

remember.meaning: Recall to mind. Remember me. Remembers, remembered, remembered.

forget.meaning: Fail to remember. Forget about it. Forgets, forgot, forgotten.

worry.meaning: Feel anxious. Worry about it. Worries, worried, worried.

enjoy.meaning: Take pleasure in. Enjoy life. Enjoys, enjoyed, enjoyed.

hate.meaning: Intensely dislike. Hate the taste. Hates, hated, hated.

love.meaning: Feel affection for. Love you. Loves, loved, loved.

fear.meaning: Be afraid of. Fear the dark. Fears, feared, feared.

hope.meaning: Wish for. Hope for success. Hopes, hoped, hoped.

time.meaning: Continuous sequence of moments. Passage of time. What is the time? (Mass noun)

person.meaning: Human individual. A person arrived. (Countable noun)

year.meaning: Period of 365/366 days. This year. (Countable noun)

way.meaning: Path/method/direction. The way home. The way to do it. (Countable noun)

day.meaning: 24-hour period or daylight. Every day. (Countable noun)

man.meaning: Adult male human. A man came. (Countable noun)

woman.meaning: Adult female human. A woman arrived. (Countable noun)

life.meaning: Existence/living things. Life is short. (Mass noun; countable in some uses)

child.meaning: Young human. The child played. (Countable noun)

world.meaning: Planet Earth/realm/domain. In the world. (Singular noun; countable in special uses)

school.meaning: Educational institution. Go to school. (Countable noun)

state.meaning: Condition/political unit. State of mind. (Countable noun)

family.meaning: Parents and children/relatives. Family is important. (Countable noun)

student.meaning: Person learning at school/university. The student studied. (Countable noun)

group.meaning: Collection of people/things. A group gathered. (Countable noun)

problem.meaning: Difficulty/question to solve. Solve the problem. (Countable noun)

hand.meaning: Limb with fingers. Hold your hand. (Countable noun)

part.meaning: Piece/component. Part of the story. (Countable noun)

book.meaning: Bound written work. Read a book. (Countable noun)

work.meaning: Activity/employment/product. Hard work. A work of art. (Mass noun when effort; countable when product)

house.meaning: Building for residence. Live in a house. (Countable noun)

number.meaning: Numerical value/quantity. A number of people. (Countable noun)

place.meaning: Location/position. A place to go. (Countable noun)

food.meaning: Edible substance. Eat food. (Mass noun; countable as 'foods')

money.meaning: Medium of exchange. Make money. (Mass noun)

tree.meaning: Large woody plant. Under the tree. (Countable noun)

love.meaning: Deep affection. Feel love. (Mass noun; countable in special uses)

friend.meaning: Person one likes. My friend. (Countable noun)

word.meaning: Linguistic unit. Say a word. (Countable noun)

language.meaning: System of communication. Speak a language. (Countable noun)

music.meaning: Organized sounds. Listen to music. (Mass noun)

name.meaning: Identification/designation. What is your name? (Countable noun)

color.meaning: Visual property. The color red. (Countable noun)

idea.meaning: Thought/concept. An idea for you. (Countable noun)

moment.meaning: Brief time period. A moment please. (Countable noun)

room.meaning: Indoor space/empty space. In the room. Make room. (Countable noun; mass in 'make room')

table.meaning: Furniture with flat top. On the table. (Countable noun)

chair.meaning: Furniture for sitting. Sit on a chair. (Countable noun)

window.meaning: Glass opening in wall. Look out the window. (Countable noun)

door.meaning: Entryway barrier. Open the door. (Countable noun)

letter.meaning: Alphabetic character/written message. Write a letter. (Countable noun)

page.meaning: Sheet in book. Turn the page. (Countable noun)

point.meaning: Specific location/purpose/degree. At this point. Make your point. (Countable noun)

law.meaning: Legal statute/natural principle. Break the law. Law of physics. (Countable noun)

song.meaning: Musical composition. Sing a song. (Countable noun)

game.meaning: Activity with rules/amusement. Play a game. (Countable noun)

sport.meaning: Athletic activity. Play a sport. (Countable noun)

war.meaning: Armed conflict. During the war. (Countable noun)

peace.meaning: Absence of conflict. Make peace. (Mass noun)

church.meaning: Religious building. Go to church. (Countable noun)

city.meaning: Large urban area. In the city. (Countable noun)

village.meaning: Small settlement. In the village. (Countable noun)

street.meaning: Road in town. On the street. (Countable noun)

road.meaning: Way for travel. Down the road. (Countable noun)

mountain.meaning: High landform. Climb the mountain. (Countable noun)

river.meaning: Large flowing water. Cross the river. (Countable noun)

field.meaning: Open land/area of study. In the field. (Countable noun)

farm.meaning: Land for agriculture. On the farm. (Countable noun)

garden.meaning: Plot for growing plants. In the garden. (Countable noun)

flower.meaning: Blooming plant. A flower grows. (Countable noun)

grass.meaning: Green plant covering. Cut the grass. (Mass noun)

fire.meaning: Combustion/flames. Make a fire. (Mass noun; countable in special uses)

light.meaning: Illumination/brightness. Turn on the light. (Mass noun; countable as fixture)

darkness.meaning: Absence of light. In the darkness. (Mass noun)

rain.meaning: Water from sky. Rainy weather. (Mass noun; countable as 'rains')

snow.meaning: Frozen water flakes. Snow fell. (Mass noun; countable as 'snows')

season.meaning: Time of year. In summer. (Countable noun)

summer.meaning: Warm season. In the summer. (Countable noun)

spring.meaning: Season of growth/coiled device. In spring. (Countable noun)

autumn.meaning: Season of decline. In autumn. (Countable noun)

morning.meaning: Early day. In the morning. (Countable noun)

afternoon.meaning: Mid-day to evening. In the afternoon. (Countable noun)

evening.meaning: End of day. In the evening. (Countable noun)

sleep.meaning: State of rest. Get sleep. (Mass noun; countable as 'sleeps')

dream.meaning: Sleep vision/aspiration. A dream came. (Countable noun)

pain.meaning: Bodily suffering. Feel pain. (Mass noun; countable as 'pains')

pleasure.meaning: Enjoyment. Give pleasure. (Mass noun; countable in special uses)

fear.meaning: Anxiety/dread. Feel fear. (Mass noun; countable as 'fears')

hope.meaning: Expectation/desire. Have hope. (Mass noun; countable as 'hopes')

truth.meaning: Conformity to fact. Tell the truth. (Mass noun; countable as 'truths')

middle.meaning: Central part. In the middle. (Countable noun)

right.meaning: Entitlement/direction. Have a right. Turn right. (Countable noun)

left.meaning: Direction/what remains. Turn left. The left side. (Countable noun)

cost.meaning: Price/expense. What is the cost? (Countable noun)

value.meaning: Worth. Great value. (Mass noun; countable as 'values')

chance.meaning: Opportunity/likelihood. A chance to go. (Countable noun)

success.meaning: Favorable outcome. Great success. (Mass noun; countable as 'successes')

failure.meaning: Lack of success. A failure occurred. (Countable noun)

beauty.meaning: Pleasing quality. Beauty of nature. (Mass noun)

health.meaning: State of wellness. Good health. (Mass noun)

illness.meaning: Disease state. A serious illness. (Countable noun)

death.meaning: End of life. Face death. (Mass noun; countable in special uses)

birth.meaning: Coming to life. Birth of a child. (Countable noun)

war.meaning: Armed conflict. During the war. (Countable noun)

peace.meaning: Absence of conflict. Make peace. (Mass noun)

government.meaning: System of authority. The government decides. (Countable noun)

president.meaning: Head of state. The president spoke. (Countable noun)

queen.meaning: Female monarch. The queen reigns. (Countable noun)

prince.meaning: Male royal offspring. The prince arrived. (Countable noun)

princess.meaning: Female royal offspring. The princess danced. (Countable noun)

soldier.meaning: Military fighter. A soldier marched. (Countable noun)

officer.meaning: Authority figure. A police officer. (Countable noun)

doctor.meaning: Medical professional. See a doctor. (Countable noun)

nurse.meaning: Healthcare provider. A nurse helped. (Countable noun)

lawyer.meaning: Legal professional. Hire a lawyer. (Countable noun)

engineer.meaning: Technical professional. An engineer designed. (Countable noun)

artist.meaning: Creative professional. The artist painted. (Countable noun)

musician.meaning: Music maker. A musician played. (Countable noun)

actor.meaning: Stage performer. The actor performed. (Countable noun)

poet.meaning: Verse composer. A poet recited. (Countable noun)

painter.meaning: Visual artist. The painter painted. (Countable noun)

farmer.meaning: Agricultural worker. The farmer planted. (Countable noun)

servant.meaning: Person who serves. The servant helped. (Countable noun)

hero.meaning: Brave person. He is a hero. (Countable noun)

villain.meaning: Evil person. The villain schemes. (Countable noun)

stranger.meaning: Unknown person. A stranger arrived. (Countable noun)

neighbor.meaning: Person living nearby. My neighbor visited. (Countable noun)

relative.meaning: Family member. A relative came. (Countable noun)

parent.meaning: Mother or father. Ask your parent. (Countable noun)

mother.meaning: Female parent. My mother is here. (Countable noun)

father.meaning: Male parent. My father is here. (Countable noun)

brother.meaning: Male sibling. My brother arrived. (Countable noun)

sister.meaning: Female sibling. My sister plays. (Countable noun)

son.meaning: Male child. My son is here. (Countable noun)

daughter.meaning: Female child. My daughter plays. (Countable noun)

husband.meaning: Married man. My husband is here. (Countable noun)

wife.meaning: Married woman. My wife arrived. (Countable noun)

boy.meaning: Young male. A boy played. (Countable noun)

girl.meaning: Young female. A girl played. (Countable noun)

foot.meaning: Extremity/unit of length. The foot is 12 inches. (Countable noun)

leg.meaning: Limb below hip. Break your leg. (Countable noun)

arm.meaning: Limb from shoulder. Raise your arm. (Countable noun)

head.meaning: Top of body. Nod your head. (Countable noun)

face.meaning: Front of head. Wash your face. (Countable noun)

nose.meaning: Olfactory organ. Your nose is here. (Countable noun)

mouth.meaning: Oral opening. Open your mouth. (Countable noun)

ear.meaning: Auditory organ. Listen with your ear. (Countable noun)

eye.meaning: Visual organ. Close your eyes. (Countable noun)

tooth.meaning: Dental structure. Brush your teeth. (Countable noun)

tongue.meaning: Oral muscle. Stick out your tongue. (Countable noun)

heart.meaning: Pumping organ/center. Follow your heart. (Countable noun)

lung.meaning: Respiratory organ. Deep breath in the lungs. (Countable noun)

brain.meaning: Thinking organ. Use your brain. (Countable noun)

bone.meaning: Skeletal structure. Break a bone. (Countable noun)

skin.meaning: Body covering. Soft skin. (Mass noun; countable as 'skins')

hair.meaning: Strands from head. Long hair. (Mass noun; countable as 'hairs')

bus.meaning: Large public vehicle. Take the bus. (Countable noun)

train.meaning: Rail-based vehicle. Ride the train. (Countable noun)

ship.meaning: Seagoing vessel. Board the ship. (Countable noun)

boat.meaning: Small vessel. Row a boat. (Countable noun)

bicycle.meaning: Two-wheeled vehicle. Ride a bicycle. (Countable noun)

horse.meaning: Large equine animal. Ride a horse. (Countable noun)

dog.meaning: Canine animal. Pet a dog. (Countable noun)

cat.meaning: Feline animal. Pet a cat. (Countable noun)

bird.meaning: Feathered animal. A bird sings. (Countable noun)

fish.meaning: Aquatic animal. Catch a fish. (Countable noun)

cow.meaning: Bovine animal. Milk a cow. (Countable noun)

sheep.meaning: Wool-bearing animal. Herd a sheep. (Countable noun)

pig.meaning: Porcine animal. Feed a pig. (Countable noun)

chicken.meaning: Poultry fowl. Raise a chicken. (Countable noun)

apple.meaning: Red/green fruit. Eat an apple. (Countable noun)

orange.meaning: Citrus fruit. Squeeze an orange. (Countable noun)

bread.meaning: Baked grain. Slice the bread. (Mass noun; countable as 'loaves' or 'breads')

cheese.meaning: Dairy solid. Slice the cheese. (Mass noun; countable as 'cheeses')

butter.meaning: Dairy fat. Spread the butter. (Mass noun)

egg.meaning: Avian reproductive cell. Crack an egg. (Countable noun)

meat.meaning: Animal flesh. Cook the meat. (Mass noun; countable as 'meats')

fish.meaning: Aquatic protein. Cook the fish. (Countable noun)

vegetable.meaning: Plant food. Eat a vegetable. (Countable noun)

fruit.meaning: Sweet plant product. Pick a fruit. (Countable noun)

grain.meaning: Cereal seed. A grain of rice. (Countable noun)

oil.meaning: Liquid fat. Cook with oil. (Mass noun)

beer.meaning: Fermented beverage. Drink beer. (Mass noun; countable as 'beers')

wine.meaning: Fermented grape drink. Pour wine. (Mass noun; countable as 'wines')

tea.meaning: Hot beverage. Drink tea. (Mass noun; countable as 'teas')

coffee.meaning: Caffeinated beverage. Drink coffee. (Mass noun; countable as 'coffees')

good.meaning: Positive quality. Good book. Comparative: better, Superlative: best.

bad.meaning: Negative quality. Bad day. Comparative: worse, Superlative: worst.

big.meaning: Large size. Big house. Comparative: bigger, Superlative: biggest.

small.meaning: Little size. Small room. Comparative: smaller, Superlative: smallest.

new.meaning: Recently made/discovered. New car. Opposite: old.

old.meaning: Not new/aged. Old house. Comparative: older, Superlative: oldest.

young.meaning: Not old/early in life. Young person. Comparative: younger, Superlative: youngest.

long.meaning: Extended length. Long road. Comparative: longer, Superlative: longest.

short.meaning: Limited length. Short story. Comparative: shorter, Superlative: shortest.

tall.meaning: Great height. Tall building. Comparative: taller, Superlative: tallest.

high.meaning: Elevated position/level. High mountain. Comparative: higher, Superlative: highest.

low.meaning: Below elevation/level. Low voice. Comparative: lower, Superlative: lowest.

hot.meaning: High temperature. Hot day. Comparative: hotter, Superlative: hottest.

cold.meaning: Low temperature. Cold weather. Comparative: colder, Superlative: coldest.

warm.meaning: Pleasantly warm. Warm coat. Comparative: warmer, Superlative: warmest.

cool.meaning: Pleasantly cold. Cool breeze. Comparative: cooler, Superlative: coolest.

fast.meaning: High speed. Fast car. Comparative: faster, Superlative: fastest.

slow.meaning: Low speed. Slow pace. Comparative: slower, Superlative: slowest.

bright.meaning: Producing light. Bright sun. Comparative: brighter, Superlative: brightest.

dark.meaning: Without light. Dark room. Comparative: darker, Superlative: darkest.

light.meaning: Not heavy. Light bag. Comparative: lighter, Superlative: lightest.

heavy.meaning: High weight. Heavy box. Comparative: heavier, Superlative: heaviest.

thick.meaning: Great depth/density. Thick book. Comparative: thicker, Superlative: thickest.

thin.meaning: Little depth/density. Thin paper. Comparative: thinner, Superlative: thinnest.

wide.meaning: Great width. Wide road. Comparative: wider, Superlative: widest.

narrow.meaning: Limited width. Narrow street. Comparative: narrower, Superlative: narrowest.

deep.meaning: Great depth. Deep water. Comparative: deeper, Superlative: deepest.

shallow.meaning: Limited depth. Shallow water. Comparative: shallower, Superlative: shallowest.

rough.meaning: Not smooth. Rough surface. Comparative: rougher, Superlative: roughest.

smooth.meaning: Having no bumps. Smooth surface. Comparative: smoother, Superlative: smoothest.

soft.meaning: Not hard. Soft pillow. Comparative: softer, Superlative: softest.

hard.meaning: Firm/solid. Hard rock. Comparative: harder, Superlative: hardest.

wet.meaning: Covered with liquid. Wet clothes. Opposite: dry.

dry.meaning: Free from moisture. Dry clothes. Opposite: wet.

clean.meaning: Free from dirt. Clean room. Opposite: dirty.

dirty.meaning: Covered with dirt. Dirty clothes. Opposite: clean.

cloudy.meaning: Not transparent/overcast. Cloudy day. Opposite: clear.

strong.meaning: Great power/force. Strong person. Comparative: stronger, Superlative: strongest.

weak.meaning: Limited power/force. Weak person. Comparative: weaker, Superlative: weakest.

sweet.meaning: Pleasant to taste. Sweet candy. Opposite: bitter.

bitter.meaning: Unpleasant to taste. Bitter medicine. Opposite: sweet.

sour.meaning: Acidic taste. Sour lemon. Opposite: sweet.

salty.meaning: Containing salt. Salty food. Opposite: bland.

bitter.meaning: Sharp/acrid taste. Bitter taste. Opposite: sweet.

quiet.meaning: Making little noise. Quiet room. Opposite: loud.

loud.meaning: Making much noise. Loud noise. Opposite: quiet.

beautiful.meaning: Pleasing to look at. Beautiful woman. Opposite: ugly.

ugly.meaning: Unpleasant to look at. Ugly building. Opposite: beautiful.

kind.meaning: Showing compassion. Kind person. Opposite: cruel.

cruel.meaning: Causing pain. Cruel person. Opposite: kind.

happy.meaning: Feeling joy. Happy person. Opposite: sad.

sad.meaning: Feeling sorrow. Sad day. Opposite: happy.

angry.meaning: Feeling rage. Angry person. Opposite: calm.

tired.meaning: Lacking energy. Tired person. Opposite: energetic.

careful.meaning: Showing caution. Careful driver. Opposite: careless.

careless.meaning: Lacking caution. Careless mistake. Opposite: careful.

stupid.meaning: Lacking intelligence. Stupid mistake. Opposite: smart.

brave.meaning: Showing courage. Brave soldier. Opposite: cowardly.

cowardly.meaning: Lacking courage. Cowardly behavior. Opposite: brave.

rich.meaning: Having much money. Rich person. Opposite: poor.

poor.meaning: Having little money. Poor family. Opposite: rich.

easy.meaning: Not difficult. Easy problem. Opposite: difficult.

difficult.meaning: Requiring effort. Difficult task. Opposite: easy.

simple.meaning: Not complex. Simple solution. Opposite: complex.

common.meaning: Frequently occurring. Common word. Opposite: rare.

rare.meaning: Seldom occurring. Rare book. Opposite: common.

similar.meaning: Having resemblance. Similar idea. Opposite: different.

different.meaning: Not the same. Different person. Opposite: similar.

other.meaning: Different/additional. Other option. Not the one mentioned.

better.meaning: Comparative form of 'good'. This is better. Superlative: best.

best.meaning: Superlative form of 'good'. The best option.

worse.meaning: Comparative form of 'bad'. This is worse. Superlative: worst.

worst.meaning: Superlative form of 'bad'. The worst day.

more.meaning: Comparative marker for long adjectives. More beautiful. For short: add -er.

most.meaning: Superlative marker for long adjectives. Most beautiful. For short: add -est.

very.meaning: To great extent/degree. Very good. Intensifier.

too.meaning: Excessively/as well. Too much. Also: in addition.

enough.meaning: Sufficient amount. Enough food. Can be adjective or adverb.

many.meaning: Large number. Many people. How many?

much.meaning: Large amount. Much water. How much?

little.meaning: Small amount/size. Little water. Little child.

several.meaning: More than two but not many. Several people.

certain.meaning: Sure/some particular. I am certain. A certain person.

real.meaning: Actually existing. Real friend. Opposite: false.

false.meaning: Not true/genuine. False claim. Opposite: true/real.

true.meaning: Conforming to fact. True story. Opposite: false.

public.meaning: Belonging to everyone. Public park. Opposite: private.

private.meaning: Belonging to individual. Private property. Opposite: public.

full.meaning: Containing all/no space left. Full bottle. Opposite: empty.

empty.meaning: Containing nothing. Empty bottle. Opposite: full.

able.meaning: Capable/competent. Able person. Able to do.

ready.meaning: Prepared/willing. Ready to go. Get ready.

unwilling.meaning: Not having will/interest. Unwilling to help.

correct.meaning: Free from error. Correct answer. Opposite: wrong.

wrong.meaning: Not correct. Wrong answer. Opposite: correct.

left.meaning: Opposite of right. Left side. Left hand.

front.meaning: At the forward side. Front door.

back.meaning: At the rear side. Back door.

first.meaning: Before others. First person. First time.

last.meaning: After all others. Last person. Last time.

next.meaning: Immediately following. Next week. Next person.

former.meaning: Belonging to an earlier period. Former president.

latter.meaning: The second of two. The latter option.

late.meaning: After the expected time. Late arrival.

temporary.meaning: Lasting a short time. Temporary job.

safe.meaning: Free from danger. Safe place. Opposite: dangerous.

dangerous.meaning: Likely to cause harm. Dangerous animal. Opposite: safe.

comfortable.meaning: Providing ease/well-being. Comfortable chair. Opposite: uncomfortable.

friendly.meaning: Showing friendship. Friendly person. Opposite: unfriendly.

polite.meaning: Showing good manners. Polite person. Opposite: rude.

rude.meaning: Lacking good manners. Rude behavior. Opposite: polite.

wise.meaning: Having good judgment. Wise decision. Opposite: foolish.

fair.meaning: Just/light-colored. Fair decision. Fair skin. Opposite: unfair.

unfair.meaning: Not just. Unfair treatment. Opposite: fair.

free.meaning: Not confined/no cost. Free person. Free food. Opposite: confined.

busy.meaning: Occupied with activity. Busy person. Opposite: idle.

inactive.meaning: Not engaged in action. Inactive person.

alive.meaning: Living. The cat is alive. Opposite: dead.

dead.meaning: No longer living. The tree is dead. Opposite: alive.

awake.meaning: Not sleeping. Awake person. Opposite: asleep.

sober.meaning: Not drunk/serious. Sober person. Sober decision.

very.meaning: To a great degree. Very good. Intensifier modifying adjectives/adverbs.

so.meaning: To such extent/degree. So good. To such a degree.

too.meaning: Excessively/as well. Too much. Also: in addition.

well.meaning: In a good manner. Do well. Opposite: badly.

badly.meaning: In a poor manner. Do badly. Opposite: well.

better.meaning: In a better manner. Do better. Comparative of 'well'.

best.meaning: In the best manner. Do your best. Superlative of 'well'.

worse.meaning: In a worse manner. Do worse.

worst.meaning: In the worst manner. Worst case.

fast.meaning: At high speed. Run fast. Opposite: slowly.

slow.meaning: At low speed. Go slow. Opposite: fast.

slowly.meaning: At low speed. Walk slowly. More formal than 'slow'.

quickly.meaning: At high speed. Leave quickly. More formal than 'fast'.

soon.meaning: In the near future. Coming soon. Temporal.

later.meaning: At a future time. See you later. Temporal.

early.meaning: Near the beginning/in advance. Arrive early. Temporal.

late.meaning: After expected time. Arrive late. Temporal.

now.meaning: At the present time. Do it now. Temporal.

then.meaning: At that time. It was then. Next/consequential.

today.meaning: On this day. See you today. Temporal.

tomorrow.meaning: On the next day. See you tomorrow. Temporal.

yesterday.meaning: On the previous day. I saw you yesterday. Temporal.

always.meaning: At all times. Always happy. Frequency.

never.meaning: Not at any time. Never seen. Frequency.

just.meaning: A moment ago/only. Just came. Just one. Time/restriction.

here.meaning: In this place. Here is it. Spatial.

there.meaning: In that place. There it is. Spatial.

everywhere.meaning: In all places. Everywhere I go. Spatial.

anywhere.meaning: In any place. Anywhere is fine. Spatial.

somewhere.meaning: In some place. Somewhere else. Spatial.

nowhere.meaning: In no place. Nowhere to go. Spatial.

back.meaning: To a previous place. Come back. Spatial/temporal.

away.meaning: To a distance. Go away. Spatial.

up.meaning: To higher place/level. Look up. Spatial.

down.meaning: To lower place/level. Sit down. Spatial.

forward.meaning: To the front. Move forward. Spatial.

backward.meaning: To the back. Move backward. Spatial.

left.meaning: To the left side. Turn left. Spatial.

right.meaning: To the right side. Turn right. Spatial.

straight.meaning: In a direct line. Go straight. Spatial.

around.meaning: In a circle/approximately. Look around. Spatial.

loudly.meaning: With loud voice. Speak loudly. Manner.

quietly.meaning: With quiet voice. Speak quietly. Manner.

softly.meaning: With soft voice. Speak softly. Manner.

gently.meaning: With gentle touch. Touch gently. Manner.

roughly.meaning: With rough touch. Handle roughly. Manner.

foolishly.meaning: With lack of wisdom. Act foolishly. Manner.

clearly.meaning: In a clear manner. Speak clearly. Manner.

specifically.meaning: In a specific way. Speak specifically. Emphasis.

mostly.meaning: In the main/mostly. Mostly true. Generality.

largely.meaning: To a great extent. Largely true. Degree.

not.meaning: Negation marker. I do not go. Negates verbs/clauses in English.

to.meaning: Infinitive marker. 'To go', 'to be'. Precedes base verb form. Different from preposition 'to'.

up.meaning: Phrasal verb particle. Get up, put up, wake up. Also: directional/completive.

down.meaning: Phrasal verb particle. Sit down, put down, write down. Also: directional/completive.

in.meaning: Phrasal verb particle. Go in, come in, fill in. Also: directional.

out.meaning: Phrasal verb particle. Go out, put out, work out. Also: directional/completive.

on.meaning: Phrasal verb particle. Turn on, put on, go on. Also: continuative.

off.meaning: Phrasal verb particle. Turn off, cut off, get off. Also: directional/completive.

away.meaning: Phrasal verb particle. Go away, give away, throw away. Also: directional/completive.

back.meaning: Phrasal verb particle. Come back, give back, look back. Also: directional/return.

over.meaning: Phrasal verb particle. Look over, turn over, go over. Also: repetition/reversal.

through.meaning: Phrasal verb particle. Go through, get through, break through. Also: completive.
